notes: Both disagreement entries name the doctrinal axis substantively. The dehāntara-prāptiḥ entry distinguishes the modern translators' choice to add 'at death' (clarifying the body-to-body transition for English readers) from Shankara's preservation of the analogical-structure-as-the-argument, and explains why translators differ in how aggressively they fill in the implied scope. The dhīraḥ entry identifies the Sanskrit's narrower technical sense (steadiness, the operator who does not flinch) vs. modern translators' generalization ('the wise'), and explains the doctrinal stake: not 'one who knows', but 'one who is not destabilized by'. Both entries pass.
